plan.action: Audit clippy.sh argument parsing: verify --dry-run, --max-retries, --workspace, --help all parse correctly and emit the expected [clippy] log lines already checked by tests/cli.bats

Add explicit WARNING log level for non-fatal issues in loop.sh and rework.sh. Merge conflict warning already uses '[clippy] WARNING:'; normalise all non-fatal advisory messages to use this prefix so operators can distinguish them from ERROR (stderr) and normal progress

Confirm retry count logging in rework_loop is surfaced per-cycle as '[rework] Verification cycle N/MAX'. Already present in rework.sh; verify format matches what tests/rework.bats and tests/loop.bats expect

Ensure loop_run_iteration logs a clear final result line on both success ('[clippy] Iteration complete') and failure ('[clippy] ERROR: Iteration failed'). These already exist in loop.sh; verify no code path silently exits without a result line
Run tests/cli.bats and tests/loop.bats to identify any currently failing tests. Fix gaps in clippy.sh or lib/loop.sh as needed until all assertions pass

Add any missing cli.bats tests for edge cases not yet covered: --max-retries with an invalid (non-numeric) value, --dry-run with a missing workspace, and the [clippy] prefix on the max-retries log line in dry-run output

testing_recommendations: tests/cli.bats: all existing tests must continue to pass — do not change test assertions, only fix the implementation
Add a test asserting --max-retries with a missing argument exits non-zero (already present) and with a non-numeric value (e.g. 'abc') exits non-zero
Add a test asserting every [clippy] WARNING: line goes to stdout (not stderr) so operators see it in the main log stream
Add a test asserting '[rework] Verification cycle N/MAX' appears in rework_loop output for both pass and fail cases
Add a test asserting loop_run_iteration emits a final result line containing either 'complete' or 'ERROR' on every code path
tests/loop.bats: verify the '--dry-run does not create LOCK file' behaviour is covered by an integration test (already exists in cli.bats); ensure no regressions in loop_run tests
Run full BATS suite after changes: bats tests/ — all 540+ tests must remain green
risks: Most acceptance criteria are already implemented; the main risk is test assertions in cli.bats relying on exact output strings that differ from current log messages — audit log lines carefully before changing them
Adding WARNING-level normalisation could break existing tests that grep for the old warning text format — update both implementation and tests atomically
bash 3.2 (macOS default) has no associative array; avoid any new data structures that depend on bash 4+ features
